easy.question: Which type of plant is the tallest?
easy.explanation: Trees are the tallest plants. They have a big trunk made of wood and many branches.

easy.question: Shrubs have many brown, woody ______ growing close to the ground.
easy.explanation: Shrubs do not have one big trunk like a tree. Instead, they have many brown, woody stems growing close to the ground.

easy.question: Which of these is a shrub?
easy.explanation: Tulsi (Holy Basil) is a shrub found in many Indian homes. It is used in prayers and medicine.

easy.question: What are herbs? Give two examples.
easy.answer: Herbs are smaller plants with soft, green stems that do not become woody. Examples are mint (pudina) and coriander (dhaniya).
easy.explanation: Herbs are much shorter than trees and shrubs and have soft, green stems.

easy.question: Which of these is the tallest grass in the world?
easy.explanation: Bamboo is the tallest grass in the world. It is a type of grass even though it looks like a tree.

medium.question: What is the difference between a tree and a shrub?
medium.answer: Trees are the tallest plants with one big trunk made of wood and deep roots. Shrubs are medium-sized plants with many brown, woody stems growing close to the ground. Shrubs look bushy.
medium.explanation: Trees have a single thick trunk while shrubs have multiple stems. Trees are taller than shrubs.

medium.question: Which tree is found in the desert areas of Rajasthan?
medium.explanation: The Khejri tree is found in the desert areas of Rajasthan.

medium.question: The grains we eat every day like rice, wheat, and bajra are all seeds of large ______.
medium.explanation: Rice (paddy), wheat, bajra, jowar, and ragi are all seeds of large grasses. Grasses are a special type of herb.

hard.question: Explain the six types of plants with one example of each.
hard.answer: The six types of plants are: 1) Trees - tallest plants with a thick trunk, like mango. 2) Shrubs - medium-sized bushy plants with many woody stems, like rose. 3) Herbs - smaller plants with soft green stems, like mint. 4) Grasses - a special type of herb with thin flat leaves and hollow stems, like wheat. 5) Climbers - plants that grow upward using support, like money plant. 6) Creepers - plants that spread along the ground, like pumpkin.
hard.explanation: Plants can be grouped into trees, shrubs, herbs, grasses, climbers, and creepers based on their size, stem type, and how they grow.

hard.question: What is the difference between a climber and a creeper? Give two examples of each.
hard.answer: Climbers are plants that grow upward by climbing walls, trees, or poles for support. Examples are money plant and jasmine. Creepers are plants that grow by spreading along the ground. Examples are pumpkin and watermelon. Some plants can behave as both climbers and creepers depending on whether they find something to climb on.
hard.explanation: Both climbers and creepers have thin, flexible stems. The key difference is the direction of growth - climbers go up using support while creepers spread on the ground.

topics.topic: Parts of a Plant and Leaves

easy.question: Which part of a plant makes food using sunlight?
easy.explanation: Leaves make food for the plant using sunlight.

easy.question: The ______ hold the plant in the soil and absorb water and nutrients.
easy.explanation: Roots hold the plant firmly in the soil and absorb water and nutrients from the ground.

easy.question: What does the stem of a plant do?
easy.answer: The stem carries water from roots to leaves and holds the plant upright.
easy.explanation: The stem acts as a pathway for water and nutrients and provides support to keep the plant standing.

easy.question: Which leaf is heart-shaped with a pointed tip?
easy.explanation: The peepal leaf is heart-shaped with a pointed tip.

easy.question: Which part of the plant contains and protects the seeds?
easy.explanation: Fruits contain and protect the seeds of a plant.

medium.question: Name any three parts of a plant and describe what each part does.
medium.answer: Roots hold the plant in the soil and absorb water. Leaves make food for the plant using sunlight. Flowers help the plant reproduce and make new plants.
medium.explanation: Every plant has six important parts: roots, stem, leaves, flowers, fruits, and seeds. Each part has a specific role.

medium.question: Bark is the hard outer ______ of a tree trunk.
medium.explanation: Bark is the hard outer covering of a tree trunk. Different trees have different bark patterns.

medium.question: Which of these leaves has a nice smell when rubbed?
medium.explanation: Mango leaves have a lovely smell when you rub them.

hard.question: How are leaves of different plants different from each other? Give examples.
hard.answer: Leaves of different plants have different colours, shapes, sizes, textures, and smells. The peepal leaf is heart-shaped with a pointed tip. The mango leaf is long and smooth with a nice smell. The lotus leaf is round and large, and water rolls off it. The khejri leaf is tiny and feathery. Some leaves like tulsi and mint have a nice smell, while others are rough or smooth to touch.
hard.explanation: Leaves come in many different shapes, sizes, colours, textures, and smells. These differences help us identify different plants.

easy.question: Which tree has aerial roots that hang down from its branches?
easy.explanation: The banyan tree has aerial roots that hang down from its branches.

easy.question: Sugar is produced from the ______ stem.
easy.explanation: Sugar is produced from the sugarcane stem. Sugarcane is a type of grass.

easy.question: What is the biggest flower in the world and where is it found in India?
easy.answer: Rafflesia is the biggest flower in the world. It is found in Mizoram and is as big as an umbrella.
easy.explanation: Rafflesia is found in Mizoram. It is the biggest flower in the world and can be as big as an umbrella.

easy.question: Which tree is known for its medicinal leaves?
easy.explanation: The neem tree is very common in India and its leaves have medicinal value.

medium.question: Name any four pulses (dal) that come from shrubs.
medium.answer: Toor (Arhar dal), Masoor (red lentils), Moong (green gram), and Urad (black gram).
medium.explanation: The pulses we eat come from shrubs. Common pulses include toor, masoor, moong, and urad.

medium.question: The Chinar tree is found in which part of India?
medium.explanation: The Chinar tree is found in Kashmir. It is known for its beautiful leaves that change colour in autumn.

hard.question: How do plants give us many useful things? List at least five things we get from plants.
hard.answer: Plants give us many useful things. 1) Food - we eat fruits, vegetables, grains, and pulses from plants. 2) Sugar - produced from sugarcane stem. 3) Medicine - plants like neem and tulsi are used to make medicines. 4) Shade - trees give us cool shade on hot days. 5) Fresh air - plants clean the air we breathe. 6) Wood - used to build houses and furniture. 7) Fibre - cotton from plants is used to make clothes.
hard.explanation: Plants are essential for human life. They provide us with food, shade, fresh air, medicine, wood, fibre, and many other useful things.
